entry.title: Behind the Scenes of Rollout Management: Key Success Factors and Lessons Learned from a Case Study
entry.content:
Behind the Scenes of Rollout Management: Key Success Factors and Lessons Learned for a Successful Implementation

Rollout management is a critical component of successfully deploying enterprise solutions like SAP. These deployments are highly complex, requiring extensive integration across various systems, business functions, and organizational units. Effective rollout management provides a structured approach to this transformative journey, minimizing disruptions, mitigating risks, and maintaining business continuity and productivity. It plays a critical role in strategic change management by fostering communication, cross-functional collaboration, comprehensive planning, and resource coordination.

This article delves into SAP’s experience in rollout management, examining key success factors and lessons learned. It aims to offer insights for organizations adopting SAP with effective strategies to ensure success.

 

A Case Study: Mastering a Complex Rollout at a Utilities Enterprise

A utility company embarked on a transformative project focusing on process harmonization, establishing an ERP core as its backbone, and leveraging a merger with another enterprise to standardize processes while transitioning to S/4HANA. The rollout spanned a six-year roadmap involving more than 400 companies, 10+ countries, and over 30,000 SAP users.

The project’s complexity demanded significant attention and dedication. By its conclusion, the global team included around 10 workstreams, 25+ teams, and over 250 employees. What were the key success factors that enabled such an extensive and successful engagement?

Effective Planning

Thorough planning and preparation are fundamental to ensuring seamless cooperation and a successful go-live. In this case, the utilities company’s rollout adopted a targeted approach with clear goals. The initial rollouts for two pilot companies served as test runs to refine subsequent rollouts based on initial lessons learned.

In order to establish a Fit-to-Standard approach, a binding S/4HANA template was established, which was as closely aligned with SAP standards as possible, given the financial framework based on the analysis of the legal gaps. The template was built with the experts from all units to cover all requirements, which were later rolled out as the standard. Any exceptional requirements to enhance the template required a clear economic rationale, and local units had to justify the added value in case there were additional requirements. Remaining close to the SAP standard minimized the need for time-consuming custom developments, ensuring a rapid and efficient rollout. This approach minimized risks, controlled scope expansion, and reduced operational costs. The pilots tested the core template and helped derive valuable experience for a broader implementation.

Furthermore, the utilities company opted for a factory approach during the rollout of the project. Each factory had its tasks and responsibilities. The Test Factory, for example, ensured that E2E processes were tested and compliant with auditing standards and automation tools. The Migration Factory focused on data migrations with a specific standardized approach and tools to scale the migrations. The Cutover Factory ensured a successful and efficient go-live by leveraging cutover and steering tools, such as cutover plans and blueprints. The Communication and Change Factory’s task was to ensure the readiness of the people by informing, activating, and enabling all users with the respective knowledge transfer.

The utilities company’s “factory approach” balanced centralized governance with local execution. Central teams managed core activities like design, testing, and migration through specialized units, ensuring consistency and quality. Meanwhile, local teams addressed region-specific needs. This model fostered collaboration between central and local efforts, streamlining delivery and aligning global standards with local requirements. It enabled scalable, efficient rollouts by establishing a lean, standardized process and tool usage and ensuring transparency of all rollout steps.

Rollout Methodology and Project Governance

The customer opted for the SAP Activate methodology utilizing SAP Solution Manager (hereafter called SolMan) / Focus Build to ensure maximum transparency and run the project in an agile mode, enabling engagement of internal resources and time-efficient delivery despite COVID-19 remote work restrictions.

In the design and realization phase, the program scope was broken into three main waves, which were delivered by scrum teams, resulting in various agile sprint teams. SAP SolMan was the single source of truth, providing transparency and using scrum boards to ensure that all topics were tracked. The project also had clear guidelines and quality criteria, such as criteria for test completion and resulting documentation. The overall agile methodology, as well as SAP SolMan, resulted in the timely delivery of qualitative results by being responsive to changing requirements.

Strong project governance was another crucial factor. Rollouts of varying sizes and scopes benefit from centralized management and logical team clusters. Efficient project management methodologies and program leadership can significantly impact outcomes.

The utilities company’s governance structure included a lean finance stream supported by parallel integration and technology streams. Agile scrum teams managed deliverables dynamically, with 20 such teams driving the project forward. Over 90% of the scope was implemented within SAP standards due to robust and strict governance.

Strong governance also facilitated risk mitigation. Centrally organized meetings provided visibility to address key challenges promptly, such as restaffing roles when necessary. The utilities company and SAP collaboratively tackled issues, ensuring success through cross-team performance and motivated staff.

Collaboration and Empowerment

Collaboration across teams and the entire project ecosystem is vital for smooth implementation. Effective change management—encompassing stakeholder management, awareness building, and resistance mitigation—creates confidence and unity among affected teams.

The utilities company prioritized collaboration by adopting the philosophy “from us for us,” engaging business, IT, central, regional teams, and partners. High levels of internal resource commitment ensured everyone actively contributed to the rollout. This large-scale engagement resulted in seamless cooperation among all parties involved.

One key lesson learned was the importance of separating program steering from content steering. Trusting system and topic owners to act in the company’s best interest proved invaluable. Addressing challenges directly as they arose ensured smooth progress. Additionally, a clear escalation procedure minimized hurdles and effort, contributing to the project’s success.

Furthermore, a key element of the project was the decision and escalation path, which vested more than 80% of the decisions by the product owners. These individuals took ownership and made decisions once unresolved topics were escalated to them. Product Managers were granted the mandate to make decisions related to cross-team matters. In this way, the product owners were empowered to take decisions and remained engaged, sharing a vested interest in the project’s success.

Driving Success in Rollout Management

The utilities company’s rollout exemplifies the principles of successful SAP implementations, especially for complex solutions like S/4HANA. Key factors included:

  • Robust Governance and Methodology: A clear governance framework and agile methodology ensured alignment, transparency, and adaptability.
  • Alignment with Standards: Staying close to SAP standards minimized complexity, reduced costs, and enhanced scalability.
  • Collaborative Engagement: Unified teams with clear roles and responsibilities fostered seamless execution and stakeholder buy-in.
  • Empowerment and Accountability: Entrusting product owners with decision-making authority accelerated progress and maintained focus on project goals.

This case study demonstrates how disciplined planning, strong governance, and collaborative culture can transform daunting challenges into scalable successes. For organizations embarking on similar transformative journeys, these strategies are not just recommended—they are essential.

The lessons learned extend beyond technology. They highlight the importance of structure, discipline, and unity in navigating complex rollouts. By adopting these principles, organizations can achieve their goals while maintaining operational excellence and driving meaningful change.

entry.title: Re: I can not create a settlement rule for some service o...
entry.content:
Well, this is a known problem (or rather "feature")... If it works from SE37, but doesn't from an RFC call, then note 206068 is your friend!
(Which by the way I created in Mar. 2000, almost 25 years ago... Man, how time goes by...)

SE37 simply does a lot of "unintuitive magic", which is not performed by the system on an incoming RFC call!

entry.title: SAP BTP Kyma headless kubeconfig with SAP Cloud Identity Services and kyma environment bindings
entry.content:
Implementing SAP Kyma headless kubeconfig with

  • a custom SAP Cloud Identity Services as SAP Kyma cluster OIDC provider
  • kymaruntime environment service bindings

Let's see how...

Table of Contents

  • kubeconfig with the authorization-code authentication flow.
  • kubeconfig with the password grant type authentication flow
  • kubeconfig with kyma runtime environment service bindings

  • References

Spoiler
https://dev.to/quovadis/sap-btp-kyma-blueprints-2cjf

Let me help break down this comprehensive information about SAP BTP Kyma kubeconfig authentication methods.

Let's understand each approach and their specific use cases.

The three main authentication methods for Kyma kubeconfig are:

1. Authorization Code Flow (OIDC)
This is the standard approach for interactive users. It requires individual users to be defined in the SAP ID tenant and have appropriate RBAC cluster-admin role bindings. While this method works well for direct user interaction, it isn't suitable for automated processes since it requires manual authentication.

2. Password Grant Type Flow
This method is designed for headless environments like CI/CD pipelines or automated workflows. It requires:
- A custom SAP Cloud Identity Service tenant
- A public client OIDC application
- A technical user with credentials stored securely
- The configuration uses a bash script to obtain tokens automatically

3. Kyma Runtime Environment Service Bindings
This approach provides service account tokens during the Kyma runtime environment provisioning.
Key points:
- Requires a cis-local plan Cloud Management service instance
- Tokens are obtained through the SAP Provisioning Service environment bindings API
- Provides the simplest configuration but requires proper setup of the environment binding

For implementing automated workflows, the Password Grant Type Flow offers the most flexibility while maintaining security through technical user credentials. The Kyma Runtime Environment Service Bindings approach is ideal when working directly with the provisioning process.

Let me know if you'd like me to elaborate on any of these authentication methods or their specific implementation details.

Spoiler

Let me explain the Password Grant Type authentication flow for SAP BTP Kyma in detail.

 

```hcl
# First, set up the Identity Application entitlement in your subaccount
resource "btp_subaccount_entitlement" "identity_application" {
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
service_name = "identity"
plan_name = "application"
}

# Reference the service plan for the identity application
data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "identity_application" {
depends_on = [btp_subaccount_entitlement.identity_application]
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
offering_name = "identity"
name = "application"
}

# Create the Identity Application service instance with specific OAuth2 configuration
resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" "identity_application" {
depends_on = [btp_subaccount_trust_configuration.custom_idp]
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
name = "ias-local"
serviceplan_id = data.btp_subaccount_service_plan.identity_application.id

# Detailed configuration for the Identity Application
parameters = jsonencode({
# Set access to public for broader accessibility
user-access = "public"

# OAuth2 configuration specifying allowed grant types and token policies
oauth2-configuration = {
grant-types = [
"authorization_code",
"authorization_code_pkce_s256",
"password", # Enable password grant type for headless authentication
"refresh_token"
],
token-policy = {
token-validity = 3600, # Access token valid for 1 hour
refresh-validity = 15552000, # Refresh token valid for 180 days
refresh-usage-after-renewal = "off",
refresh-parallel = 3,
access-token-format = "default"
},
public-client = true, # Enable public client mode
redirect-uris = [
"https://dashboard.kyma.cloud.sap",
"http://localhost:8000"
]
},

# Configure how user identifiers are determined
subject-name-identifier = {
attribute = "mail",
fallback-attribute = "none"
},

# Map assertion attributes to specific user properties
assertion-attributes = {
email = "mail",
groups = "companyGroups",
first_name = "firstName",
last_name = "lastName",
login_name = "loginName",
mail = "mail",
scope = "companyGroups",
user_uuid = "userUuid",
locale = "language"
},

# Set the application name using variables
name = "${var.BTP_KYMA_NAME}-${var.BTP_KYMA_PLAN}-${data.btp_subaccount.context.id}",
display-name = "${var.BTP_KYMA_NAME}-${var.BTP_KYMA_PLAN}"
})
}

```

The Password Grant Type authentication flow is designed specifically for non-interactive, automated scenarios. Let me break down how it works and why each component is important:

The Setup Process:

1. Custom Identity Service Setup
- You need to create a custom SAP Cloud Identity Service (IAS) tenant rather than using the default SAP ID Service
- This gives you more control over authentication policies and user management
- The service instance is configured as a public client, which is crucial for the password grant flow

2. OAuth2 Configuration
- The setup enables multiple grant types, but specifically includes the "password" grant
- Token policies are configured with specific validities:
- Access tokens last for 1 hour (3600 seconds)
- Refresh tokens last for 180 days (15552000 seconds)
- The public client setting eliminates the need for client secrets

3. User Attribute Mapping
- The configuration maps various user attributes (email, groups, name, etc.)
- This mapping ensures that Kubernetes RBAC can properly identify and authorize users
- The "mail" attribute serves as the primary identifier for users

Using the Authentication:

When this is set up, your automated processes can authenticate using a technical user's credentials. Here's how it works:

1. The process makes a POST request to the IAS token endpoint
2. It provides:
- The technical user's username and password
- The client ID of your IAS application
- The required scopes (typically "groups" and "email")
3. The IAS service returns an ID token
4. This token is then used for Kubernetes authentication

Security Considerations:

1. Technical User Management
- Create a dedicated technical user in your IAS tenant
- Assign minimum required permissions through groups
- Regularly rotate the password
- Store credentials in a secure vault or secret management system

2. Token Handling
- Never log or expose the tokens
- Implement proper error handling for token expiration
- Use refresh tokens when appropriate to minimize password usage

3. Access Control
- Implement proper RBAC policies in Kubernetes
- Regularly audit access patterns
- Monitor for suspicious authentication attempts

This authentication method is particularly useful for:
- CI/CD pipelines
- Automated deployment scripts
- Monitoring systems
- Backup solutions
- Any other headless processes that need to interact with your Kyma cluster

Would you like me to elaborate on any particular aspect of this setup, such as the technical user creation process or specific security best practices?

 

Spoiler

Let me explain the Kyma Runtime Environment Bindings method, which provides a streamlined way to obtain cluster access through SAP BTP service bindings.

 

```hcl
# First, set up the Cloud Management Service instance
resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" "cis_service" {
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
name = "cis-local"
serviceplan_id = data.btp_subaccount_service_plan.cis_local.id
}

# Create the service binding for Cloud Management Service
resource "btp_subaccount_service_binding" "cis_binding" {
depends_on = [btp_subaccount_service_instance.cis_service]
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
service_instance_id = btp_subaccount_service_instance.cis_service.id
name = "cis-local-binding"
}

# Set up the Kyma runtime environment
resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" "kyma_runtime" {
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
name = var.kyma_instance_name
serviceplan_id = data.btp_subaccount_service_plan.kyma_runtime.id

parameters = jsonencode({
name = var.kyma_instance_name
planName = var.kyma_plan_name
shootName = var.shoot_name
targetK8sVersion = var.kubernetes_version
machineType = var.machine_type
autoScalerMin = var.min_nodes
autoScalerMax = var.max_nodes
maxSurge = 1
volumeSizeGb = var.volume_size
diskType = "pd-standard"
region = var.region
workerCidr = var.worker_cidr
servicesCidr = var.services_cidr
podsCidr = var.pods_cidr
})
}

# Create the service binding for Kyma runtime
resource "btp_subaccount_service_binding" "kyma_binding" {
depends_on = [btp_subaccount_service_instance.kyma_runtime]
subaccount_id = data.btp_subaccount.context.id
service_instance_id = btp_subaccount_service_instance.kyma_runtime.id
name = "${var.kyma_instance_name}-binding"
}

```

The Kyma Runtime Environment Bindings method works through the SAP BTP service binding mechanism. Let me break down how this approach functions and why it's particularly useful.

Understanding the Components:

1. Cloud Management Service (CIS)
This service acts as a bridge between your BTP subaccount and the Kyma runtime. The CIS instance:
- Manages service credentials
- Handles authentication and authorization
- Provides necessary APIs for runtime management
- Must be provisioned with the cis-local plan

2. Kyma Runtime Instance
The Kyma runtime itself needs specific configuration, including:
- Kubernetes version specifications
- Node pool configurations
- Network settings
- Resource allocations
- Regional preferences

3. Service Bindings
Two key bindings are created:
- CIS binding: Provides access to cloud management capabilities
- Kyma binding: Delivers the actual kubeconfig credentials

How the Authentication Works:

When you create a service binding to your Kyma runtime:
1. The BTP Provisioning Service generates a service account in your Kyma cluster
2. This service account receives appropriate RBAC permissions
3. A kubeconfig file is generated containing a bearer token
4. The token is automatically rotated and managed by the platform

Advantages of This Method:

1. Simplified Management
- No need to manage separate credentials
- Platform handles token rotation automatically
- Reduced operational overhead
- Integrated with BTP service management

2. Security Benefits
- Tokens are managed by the platform
- Clear audit trail through BTP
- Automatic credential rotation
- Integrated with BTP security controls

3. Operational Efficiency
- Direct integration with BTP services
- No additional authentication setup required
- Works seamlessly with other BTP services
- Easy to automate through infrastructure as code

Best Practices for Implementation:

1. Service Instance Names
- Use consistent naming conventions
- Include environment indicators
- Make names descriptive and meaningful
- Document naming patterns

2. Binding Management
- Create separate bindings for different purposes
- Monitor binding status
- Implement proper error handling
- Keep binding information secure

3. Access Control
- Review generated RBAC permissions
- Monitor service account usage
- Implement proper security policies
- Regular access audits

Common Use Cases:

This method is particularly useful for:
- Development environments integrated with BTP
- Automated deployment pipelines
- Platform operations tools
- Monitoring and logging systems
- Any scenario where tight BTP integration is desired

The bindings method provides a more automated and platform-integrated approach compared to other authentication methods. Would you like me to elaborate on any specific aspect, such as the binding creation process or how to manage multiple environments?

entry.title: SAP HANA : Cannot create Instance
entry.content:
Hi, I am trying to install SAP HANA using the MS Azure VM (open suse 15 )
But not able to install
I am getting below error

 


Installing components...
Configuring SAP HANA Database...
Configuring System...
Configuring instance...
Resume of SAP HANA Express Edition Installation failed
Installation of SAP HANA Database failed
Installation failed
error installing

SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud  SAP HANA Cloud

and no issue for production

production order status released

production order type special

How to cancel two  Receipt from Production in this case without variance

Please, advice

Many thanks
Salah35

entry.title: Placing Attachment on SFTP server using Integration Suite Iflow
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Is it possible to place message attachment on SFTP server instead of body payload using Receiver SFTP adapter in Integration suite?

Thanks,

Nilesh

entry.title: Re: In script hyphen is updating as hashtag
entry.content: So, you convert to PDF and it's in the PDF that you have the hash character(s). How do you convert to PDF? For information, internally, an output device type is always used even if you don't print. The logic if very complex, so you should give all possible details (debug and so on). There are many existing questions and SAP notes which explain how PDF conversion works in the many scenarios.

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content:
I have narrowed it down to my repeated values not being assigned by my database's REST API and AppGyver's Data Resource.  The "Set repeated item value" flow function was not the problem, that works perfectly as you found also.

I now think the new UI handles REST API's differently (perhaps more stringently) so I am trying to tighten up my code to deal with it. 

Thanks Dan for looking into it.

Nick

entry.title: Re: Stop Guessing! Use Case with Conversational AI Agents in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: In the case of HANA tool, you shown the agent queries the database using a technical user and not the business end-user. How do you ensure that the agents do not provide data that the user is not supposed to access ?

entry.title: Employee Central Exam Attempt
entry.content:
Good Evening Everyone

May I please get assistance and guidance on how I can pass the Employee Central Exam

entry.title: Re: Triger workflow for time off requests
entry.content:
Thanks, PetraRana.

Yes, I found the solution. 

At first, I thought the solution could use rule in Job Info Bussiness Configuration but I solved that by take rule for time type.

entry.title: RMAN-06094: datafile 612 must be restored, issue in recovery of standby Oracle database
entry.content:
Recently during a on-premise to Azure migration of SAP NetWeaver running on Oracle, an issue was observed while attempting to perform a recovery of the Oracle database after the backup was successfully restored.

During the roll forwarding by applying the offline redologs, after a sequence was applied the below shown error interrupted the recovery even though offline redologs were available for further point in time  recovery

RMAN-03002: failure of recover command at 12/21/2024 22:57:04

RMAN-06094: datafile 612 must be restored

After thoroughly checking the logs it was found that a new data file corresponding to the number 612 was created in the source DB after the backup was taken, hence it was not a part of the restore.

Solution:

  • First we need to locate the path of the new data file in the source system.

         Login to the sqlplus of the source database and execute the below query:         

 

SELECT FILE#, NAMEFROM V$DATAFILEWHERE FILE# = 612;

 

  • Create the folder based upon the path taken from the previous SQL query

         Login to the target DB and create the below folder         

 

mkdir /oracle/SID/sapdata1/sr3_539

 

  • Run the command to create the data file to the newly created path

         In the target database, login to RMAN and issue the below command         

 

RUN {SET NEWNAME FOR DATAFILE 612 TO '/oracle/<SID>/sapdata1/sr3_539/sr3.data539';
RESTORE DATAFILE 612;}

 

  • Switch the datafile

         In the target database, login to RMAN and issue the below command    

 

SWITCH DATAFILE 612 TO COPY;

 

  • Issue the recovery command

         In the target database, login to RMAN and issue the below command

 

recover database;

 

The remaining offline redologs start to get applied in the target database and roll forwarding completes.

Conclusion: If a new data file is created in the source database after the backup has been taken and the same backup is used to restore and recover the database on target then to ensure the point in time recovery, a corresponding blank data file needs to be created in the same path as it is created in source to facilitate roll forwarding by application of offline redologs.

entry.title: Re: How to process the delta records in SAP Datasphere?
entry.content: Hello, how can you load just the delta records in a different table? Are you using replication flows?

entry.title: Re: Insufficient Resources Error while deploying in CF (B...
entry.content: Indeed, yesterday I experienced the same issue. Today (morning) it was working fine again. Now it's not working anymore. Strange behaviour, not very stable environment I have the impression.

entry.title: native sql question
entry.content:
Hello experts on line, my codes is as below and without syntax error.

TYPES: BEGIN OF t_str,

carrid like sflight-carrid,

connid like sflight-connid,

END OF t_str.

data: gt_itab type table of t_str,

gs_wa type t_str,

gv_carrid like sflight-carrid value 'AA'.

EXEC SQL PERFORMING APPEND_ITAB.

SELECT A.CARRID, A.CONNID

INTO :GS_WA-CARRID, :GS_WA-CONNID

FROM SFLIGHT A

WHERE A.MANDT =: SY-MANDT AND A.CARRID =: GV_CARRID

ENDEXEC.

FORM APPEND_ITAB.

WRITE:/ GS_WA-CARRID, GS_WA-CONNID.

APPEND GS_WA TO GT_ITAB.

CLEAR GT_ITAB.

ENDFORM.

But when it runs, it has runtime error in category installation error. Short Text is ‘SQL error 257 occured while executing Native SQL’. How can I correct it? Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to Handle Empty Data Arrays in MDK App to Prevent Crashes?
entry.content: What platform are you working with?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAL S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance
entry.content: According to the information in the URL, [SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01] was updated from "Fiori configuration only" to "fully activated appliance" in the second quarter of 2024. As for [SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02], in the CAL appliance template list, FPS02 is also displayed as "fully activated appliance". However, when checking the information in the URL, as of the fourth quarter of 2024, it is "Fiori configuration only", so information is currently being collected.

entry.title: Re: Apps for spool requests in SAP Public cloud
entry.content: Thank you for your response! I will indeed take a look at the provided notes!

entry.title: Re: ES version, if there are more than 100,000 fixed asse...
entry.content: 感谢您的帮助和指导！

entry.title: Execute Direct HSQL Queries
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In our on-premise environment we were generating excel report while executing direct queries to the database. See the attached screen shot.

Can you please share Cloud DB details so that we can configure and generate the same report from our new ccv2-p1 environment?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Execute Direct HSQL Queries
entry.content: raise a new ticket to get data of database from ccv2 to your local database then generate the report in your local or using exist api or customize your own api on ccv2 to get the useful data.

entry.title: Forms KPIs report
entry.content:
Upon clicking on the Analytics tab in a Form.

You will see KPIs, Views, Submission, Submission/Views. New Contacts, and New Contacts/Submissions

We want to have same report as Form Analytics KPIs.
However we can not find the fields that are shown in Analytics in standard CDS views or standard analytics reports.
Could you tell us which CDS views are used to generate the KPIs in Form ?
What we're looking for is following.
1. Is "Views" KPI coming from the interaction that have Interaction type=Click_through? If so the interactions are generated campaign level, not Form.
   How/from which CDS Form Analytics get the value?
2. Is "Submitted" the interaction that have Interaction type = WEBSITE_REGISTRATION ? How/from which CDS Form Analytics get the value?
3. How "New Contact" identify new contact? What is the definition of "New"?

entry.title: Customize Custom Fields group and making it show in add new class page
entry.content:
Hi All

1) would like to know if there are ways to set LMS custom field in Class to show in seperate group out side "Custom fields" group. For example, "Evaluation" group/segment that only contain selected custom fields about evaluation score.

2) Can we make selected custom field show in "Add new" page when we create new class?

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud (Grow) - option to develop simple APPs
entry.content:
Hi,

You need Business Application Studio to publish your APP. If you want to know more about developing an APP in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can read these blogs:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, ABAP Environmen... - SAP Community

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, ABAP Environmen... - SAP Community

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: how to create a Custom Widget Sankey Chart in SAC?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to develop a Custom Widget for a Sankey Chart for SAP Analytics Cloud.
I am using reference from this blog but the preexisting on from there does suffice my needs.
I need to modify or develop a new script to render a Node Aligned Sankey .

But with all my own developments or modifications to SAP scripts I am always getting the error : Something went wrong. The system couldn't load the custom widget 'com.sap.sac.sample.echarts.sankeyyg_1.x' (kind: 'main') for this reason: The system took too long to define the custom widget. Please contact your system administrator or the owner of the custom widget. 
and then
Failed to retrieve constructor for custom widget 'com.sap.sac.sample.echarts.sankeyyg_1.x' (kind: main)

can you please help/advice me on how to either resolve these errors or develop the charts?

entry.title: Adding a new checklist step in existing checklist profile
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am currently adding a new config of Checklist requirement under an existing checklist profile. The requirement is to add 'Review ABAP/UI Deployment Sheet' as an additional checklist under 'Solution Documentation Review'. So the thing is, when this checklist is selected, the step 'Review Solution Documentation' automatically shows under it, however, the 'Review ABAP/UI Deployment Sheet' needs to be added manually as the second requirement. Does anyone know where I can fix this for it to get automatically shown?

Also, after a few other changes in SPRO specifically in Cat. Item Usage etc., I encounter below errors when creating a new Normal Change to be used for testing purposes.

Error Customizing of item cat. or item cat. usage required for all steps

Error Item 8000000010: Current and previous step number cannot be identical

Error Item 8000000010: Cyclical step order not permitted

Warning Message Item 8000000010: Enter a partner ID for checklist step

 

I'm possible thinking I should revert some changes performed as I did not encounter these errors the first time I did some changes.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Hosting CodeJam in Chennai India BTP and ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Ajiths ,

Jan 24th is not possible as we have ABAP CodeJam planned in Hyderabad. I’ll set up a call with you in first week of January to discuss the next possible dates.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Flexible WF PR
entry.content:
In the public cloud for PR release strategy, using a flexible workflow is a must. They have given only three parameters for release (Company code, document type, creation ind. and amount). At my customer, we need material group, etc also. But there seems to be no option to add more parameters. If we go only with the given parameters too about 50+ document types will be created

We need the way out for the following.

1. Can we add custom parameters?

2. If a user is authorized for 3 PR document types, will he see all the PRs in his drop-down or only 3?


Your usual support is appreciated.

 

 

 

Best Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud Flexible WF PR
entry.content:
Dear Customer, Please find more details about the workflow from the url below, BAdI and custom logic can be added for custom conditions to trigger the workflow.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/bacc21e14d6c4b0fbf53dca3218abdd5.html?locale=en-US&q=workflow%20purchase%20requisition

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/8536fac83ec045d796cb6f12a5f21bf1.html?locale=en-US&q=workflow%20purchase%20requisition

And for the restricition, it will only restrict the transactional data but not the data int he drop-list.

entry.title: Re: Create dropdown in selection screen
entry.content:
TYPE-POOLS : VRM.

DATA: p_name TYPE vrm_id,
      list   TYPE vrm_values,
      value  LIKE LINE OF list.
DATA: gwa_list    TYPE vrm_value.
DATA: gt_values  TYPE TABLE OF dynpread,
      gwa_values TYPE dynpread.
******************************************************

PARAMETERS : p_prctr     TYPE bsis-prctr  MODIF ID prk  AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.

**********************************************

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON p_prctr.
  CLEAR: GWA_VALUES, gt_values.
  REFRESH gt_values.
GWA_VALUES-fieldname = 'P_PRCTR'.
  APPEND GWA_VALUES TO GT_VALUES.
  CALL FUNCTION 'DYNP_VALUES_READ'
    EXPORTING
      dyname             = sy-cprog
      dynumb             = sy-dynnr
      translate_to_upper = 'X'
    TABLES
      dynpfields         = gt_values.

  READ TABLE gt_values INDEX 1 INTO gwa_values.
  IF sy-subrc = 0 AND gwa_values-fieldvalue IS NOT INITIAL.
    READ TABLE list INTO gwa_list
                      WITH KEY key = gwa_values-fieldvalue.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
*      gv_selected_value = gwa_list-text.
      MOVE gwa_list-text TO p_prctr.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

***********************************************************

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
IF list[] IS INITIAL.
  p_name = 'P_PRCTR'.
  value-key = '1'.
  value-text = '5000'.
  APPEND value TO list.

  value-key = '2'.
  value-text = '5003'.
  APPEND value TO list.

  value-key = '3'.
  value-text = '5004'.
  APPEND value TO list.

  value-key = '4'.
  value-text = '5005'.
  APPEND value TO list.

  value-key = '5'.
  value-text = '5007'.
  APPEND value TO list.

  value-key = '6'.
  value-text = '5010'.
  APPEND value TO list.

  CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING id = p_name values = list.

ENDIF.

entry.title: Corssboarding - Triggering I-9 Compliance form for International - Internal hires moving into the US
entry.content:
We are running into a scenario where I-9 forms are not triggering for internal hires, who are moving from an international country into the United States.

These are existing employees who went through a recruiting selection process, and were selected for a new job within the US.  Global Mobility is not being used.  They are keeping the same employee ID numbers.  They are not being terminated. They were hired using SF Recruiting and are being moved into SF Onboarding module for a Cross-boarding process. They will switch from their previous job outside of the US to a new job within the US.

I-9 Compliance form is triggering for US hires switching jobs.

The I-9 Compliance form is not triggering when an existing international employee is an internal hire and is moving to anew job in the US.

Has anyone dealt with a similar scenario, and if so where there any unique configuration settings, permission updates, business rules, or crossboarding settings that you can suggest we attempt to replicate?

Best,

Drew

entry.title: Re: Amount field length extension in S4HC
entry.content:
Limitations:

This extension option is not availablein SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. Currently, there is no plan to make it available. That means that our customers are only able to enter the maximum number of 13 characters in Finance documents and 11 characters, on line item level, in Logistic documents. Example: The maximum amount for invoice line items in Indonesia is 99,999,999,999 IDR (11 digits), which equals 6,000 EUR.

Recommended Approach:

Conduct the following steps .

1.Assess in how far your customer belongs to one of the following segments

–High inflation / low value currency countries like Indonesia, Venezuela, Colombia, and Vietnam
–High amount industry like Financial Services, Public Sector, Mining, Airline Industry, Telco, Real Estate and Construction
–If yes, familiarize yourself with SAP Note 2628654 and check field length requirements of your customer:–Currency amount field length in Finance
–Currency amount field length in Logistics–Exchange rate field length

2.In addition, check for installed base customers of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, in your customer segment and region. This will help to understand the relevance of the field length topic

3.Progress with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, in case you have not identified any issues from 1., 2.

4.Check the number of documents affected in a given timeframe and check if a manual workaround (split into multiple documents) would be acceptable

5.Reach out to DL PCAP PM team if further clarification is needed

6.In case of remaining concerns progress with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Here you have the option to activate the extension of field length

entry.title: Re: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:

very bad experience please don't waste your time on it

entry.title: Re: Alternate CDS for I_StatusObjectStatusChange
entry.content:
Hi,

To retrieve system status data similar to the functionality provided by the CDS view I_StatusObjectStatusChange, you can explore the following options with SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

1. I_StatusObjectStatus: This CDS view might offer details about the current status of objects and could serve as an alternative to obtain information similar to what is stored in tables JEST (which holds the object status at runtime).

2. I_StatusAction: While this view might not provide historical data (like JCDS, which stores change documents for status objects), it can give insight into status actions associated with objects.

Unfortunately, the provided context does not list specific details about CDS views related to JEST or JCDS directly, so you may want to explore these CDS views within your SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment to evaluate if they meet your requirements.

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: Re: SAP List Viewer with Integrated Data Access (ALV with IDA)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" ALV with IDA (SAP List Viewer with Integrated Data Access) helps tables that contain very large quantities of data to be displayed on the UI. The results of operations such as sorting, grouping, or filtering are also delivered with a very fast response time. It uses the in-memory database, such as SAP HANA, without having to switch to a new programming environment. There is no change in the User interface and standard functions (also ALV services). ALV functions are adjusted to the use of in-memory databases. The new general programming model (Coding Pushdown) is also optimally supported when using in-memory databases. "

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, 

it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Graph Workspace with LangChain
entry.content: Very neat! Thanks, Sandesh. Keep writing. 🌟

entry.title: How to resent COF welcome email if customer doesn't receive before.

Hi

As the subject, I need to send the COF Welcom email to customer again. How could I do that?

entry.title: SAP Private Link Service connect to Azure Private Link Service does not work
entry.content:
Hi,

I have an Azure resource group with below topology:

- A VM with a simple web server at port 80.

- An internal Load Balancer in front of the VM. A load balancing rule is created to route from load balancer's IP and port 80 to VM's IP and port 80.

- A Private Link Service attached with the Load Balancer.

I followed the tutorials at https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/private-link-microsoft-azure.html to create a Private Endpoint from my BTP subaccount to Azure Private Link Service successfully. An approved Private Endpoint is created. I also bound my BTP application with my Private Link Service instance.

According to the document at https://help.sap.com/docs/private-link/private-link1/azure-private-link-service-generic-lb-scenario-for-vms-and-others, I should be able to call the VM's web page through Load Balancer's IP at port 80 from my BTP application. The call should go through Private Endpoint to resolve the VM's IP and I can open the connection from my BTP application. However, I got connection refused error in this case.

Could any one please give me some advice? Maybe I misconfigure something on subaccount or Azure side.

Thanks,

Steven

entry.title: Restrict User to make changes to Payroll Area effective start date of next Pay Period
entry.content:
We have to create a business rule under Compensation Information, so that changes made to Payroll Area are effective Begin date of next Pay Period.

In order to achieve that we have created a Variable to Get Start Date of Next Pay Period(). And under If Expression applied a condition that "

If

Compensation Information Model.Payroll Area.Value is not equal to Compensation Information Model.Payroll Area.Previous value 

And

Compensation Information Model.Event Date. Value is not equal to Variable (var_Begin date of Next pay Period))

Then

Raise "Error Message"

But, we are not able to get Start Date of Next pay Period with the help of this variable. May I know if any one has applied a business rule to achieve this. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Shell Plugin for Admin Site Portal- SCP
entry.content: Creating a shell plugin for the admin site portal in SCP requires careful planning and alignment with the SAP Fiori launchpad plugin guidelines. Here are some steps you can follow: Understand the Plugin Requirements: Determine the specific features or functions you want to add through the shell plugin. For instance, is it for navigation, UI enhancement, or integrating additional functionalities? Set Up Your Development Environment: Make sure your environment is ready with the necessary tools, including SAP Business Application Studio or WebIDE, depending on your subscription. Create the Shell Plugin Project: Use the "New Project from Template" option in your development tool. Select "SAP Fiori Launchpad Plugin." Configure the plugin with the required namespace and IDs. Write the Custom Code: Add the custom JavaScript to extend or override default shell functionalities. You can interact with the Launchpad using the sap.ushell library. Deploy and Test: Deploy your plugin to your SCP subaccount and test it within the admin portal. Ensure proper integration without affecting the core functionality of the portal. Secure and Optimize: Double-check role configurations to ensure only authorized users can access or modify the plugin. Also, optimize your code for performance.

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Graph Workspace with LangChain
entry.content: Good one, expecting more such blogs from you Sandesh! 👍

entry.title: Re: How to resent COF welcome email if customer doesn't r...
entry.content:
@Huber wrote:

Hi

As the subject, I need to send the COF Welcom email to customer again. How could I do that?

Hello @Huber,

Typically, you can resend a confirmation email directly through your email service provider or customer management system. Here’s a quick guide: flvs flex login

  1. Log in to your email service provider or customer management system.

  2. Navigate to the "Sent" or "Outbox" folder to find the original COF Welcome email.

  3. Open the email and look for a "Resend" or "Forward" option.

  4. Modify the recipient's information if necessary, then click "Send."

If your system doesn’t have a direct resend feature, simply forward the original email and include any additional context the customer might need.

Best Regards,
James Henry

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA : Cannot create Instance
entry.content:
Hi Sriram, Thanks for you reply.

I found the issue and resolved that issue. 
In the log file it mentioned one library not present (libltdl.so.7 not found) 
I downloaded the RPM file for above library and installed. 
Now it is working fine.

entry.title: Re: How to Handle Empty Data Arrays in MDK App to Prevent...

entry.title: Modify one column of internal table using variable value
entry.content:
If you are trying to modify one or more columns of internal table by using variable value 

follow the below method to do it.

Declare work area (structure) of that same internal table 
Assign value of variable to the specific column. ex: ls_sample-name = lv_name_var

MODIFY lt_internal_tab FROM ls_sample TRANSPORTING name (column names) WHERE some condition.

ls_main_new_data-demd_n_due_date = lv_new_dn_duedate.
ls_main_new_data-bli_due_date = lv_new_bli_due.
*
MODIFY lt_new_demand_main FROM ls_main_new_data TRANSPORTING bli_due_date demd_n_due_date
 WHERE bli_due_date = '00000000' AND demd_n_due_date = '00000000' .

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA service - requote a service quation with refe...
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

Thanks for your detailed explanation of the scenario. I am able to follow. You are also right that requote in its current form will create new quotation item and new billable maintenance order.

However, I would like to clarify with you few aspects:

1. Have you tried to check whether "Price item" fits your requirements ? Please check this new feature which is already available, it might support your use case to some extent : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c9b5e9de6e674fb99fff88d72c352291/ac29fa738c424fb2adca26e6475b3310.html 

2. What is the motivation to extend the operations and components in the same billable maintenance order ? Why is it not possible to use a second maintenance order with a second item ? Please note that it will still be part of the same service order.

Kindly help me to understand this so that we could consider your feedback in future roadmap.

 

Thanks and Kind Regards,

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: Long term planning
entry.content:
LTP (Long Term Planning) takes into account assigned PIR (Planned Independent Requirements) no matter if their 'Active' Indicator is set or not. MRP only looks at active PIR.

Thus: Reset the 'Active' Flag in the PIR which are intended for simulation only (transaction MD62).

entry.title: Maximum Limit on Rebate | Settlement Management | Condition Contract
entry.content:
Is it possible to achive the below objectives with standard configuration

1.Restrict the settlment amount for a rebate with a maximum value?

Example: Rebate of  5% maintained in the conditions, but the maximum rebate a customer can recieve should be restricted with a value say 1000$ 

2.Rebate should only apply on the quantity after the scale value?

Example: Rebate of 5 % is maintained in the condiitons with a scale of 100EA. When the business volume is 120EA, the rebate of 5 % should apply only on 20EA and not on the 120 EA.

entry.title: Integration for SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
hi Expert
I would like to ask if there is a way in SAP Digital Manufacturing to retrieve user information, user Clock in /out data , and skill information in real time from a third-party application without integrating with SuccessFactors. Additionally, is there a way for managers to view the daily attendance records of all direct employees?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) a
entry.content:
@Subhram_Singh 
First, I want to clarify that Content Agent service (CAS) service instance or UI subscription is required from Source account that is your DEVCI or DEVHR account. And in these source accounts you also need the CloudIntegration destination pointing to the CPI locally and a destination pointing to the Central Cloud Transport Management. 
Regarding your query on having two separate destinations for CloudIntegration in the same account. That wont be required when you use CAS from source account instead of TMS account. 

An alternate solution is to use the UI from Content Agent UI which needs to be subscribed again only in source accounts. The destination set up for CloudInteration remains the same. You can know more on this by following the blog - Transport selected artefact to Cloud Transport Management service using Content Agent UI.

entry.title: Re: Understanding SAP Versions
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" SAP revised the naming convention in 2020. Going forward, On-Premise releases will be named for the year only (YYYY), starting with S/4HANA 2020 in October 2020. Cloud releases will continue with the YYMM naming convention. "

 

Thanks for your kindly sharing, it really help us a lot.

Regards,

🙂

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Conditional replication in mdg.bp
entry.content:
Hi Srinivas,

You can look at the standard MDG help documentation to look for the standard filters available for business partners.

There are standard segment filters available out of the box which can be used to filter the data.

Set Up Data Replication Framework (DRF) | SAP Help Portal

By default, the replication timing is during final check approved and it can be controlled at the outbound implementation settings in DRFIMG.

 

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Identity Services - IPS ( How to Assign default groups and Unassign groups on Conditio
entry.content:
Hi Yogananda,
the "targetVarible": "assignGroup" is apparently currently not extendable. 
If i want to fill the list based on multiple conditions I'd have to write a JSON block for each possible scenario to have an exact match, like:
if X -> Group A 
if Y -> Group B
if Z -> Group C
if YZ -> Group B,C
if ZX > Group A,C
Instead of beeing able to fill a list like the groups[] or BusinessRoles[] for other system types, do you know about any other solution here?

regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: How to find the history of approved or rejected form...
entry.content:
Hi @arroyoweisson,

Thanks for sharing the information about process visibility scenario.

I checked about it and I found that the log will be automatically delete after 14 days.

And the requirement we would like to get is that we want to keep the history until admin delete the history.

So, is it other possible way to keep that so?

To do this so, we will need to keep the history in external storage.

And do you have any documentation about keeping history in external storage?

 

Thank You.

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: sap analytics cloud data action advanced formula to d...
entry.content:
Hi @hsthind

A simple solution is to wrap it with "FOREACH [d/CostCenter]" for a 15 and 16-line script. However, this may cause performance degradation.

You can achieve the expected result for all cost centers using the script below.

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "Amount"
// MEMBERSET [d/CostCenter] = "CC01"
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = "202401" TO "202412"
MEMBERSET [d/GLAccount] = "GL01"

VARIABLEMEMBER #NO_Week OF [d/GLAccount]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Sum_OF_Valu OF [d/GLAccount]

IF MOD(MONTH([d/Date]), 3) = 0 THEN
	DATA([d/GLAccount] = #NO_Week, [d/CostCenter] = "#") = 5
ELSE
	DATA([d/GLAccount] = #NO_Week, [d/CostCenter] = "#") = 4
ENDIF

DATA([d/GLAccount] = #Sum_OF_Valu, [d/Date] = "202412") = RESULTLOOKUP()
DATA() = RESULTLOOKUP([d/GLAccount] = #Sum_OF_Valu, [d/Date] = "202412") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/GLAccount] = #NO_Week, [d/CostCenter] = "#") / 52

 

Regards,

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition without Valuation Price
entry.content:
Hello  Haily:

For this requirement you can use BADI:MM_PUR_S4_PR_FLDCNTRL_SIMPLE to control field properties, Please check this OSS:https://me.sap.com/notes/3345421

Best Regards

Kane

entry.title: Re: How to create BS flows from FF67 with Flow Builder Plus?
entry.content: Hello, Would you check if using the Fiori app 'Manage Bank Statement' to manually create a bank statement flow instead of using the T-code FF67? Kind regards, Vincent

entry.title: Re: Entity-driven Enterprise Architecture using SAP Graph
entry.content: Good day, is the presentation available to view? Would like to now more about the subject.

entry.title: Budget for Cost Center Check On PR is Unavailable
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are working with the budget for cost center. We already configure the budget availability control for cost center and it works perfectly on purchase order.

But the budget check does not work on purchase requisition. Is it standard behavior ? or there are some configuration that we missed?

Thank you!

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: How do we configure sampling procedures for pharma?
entry.content:
Greetings, 

the following master data is required

You need a sampling procedure for physical samples (QDV1)

You need a sample drawing procedure which supports partial samples (QPV2)

You need an inspection plan which combines sampling procedure, sample drawing procedure and inspection characteristics (QP01)

In my example I have prepared two operations which could so respond to two laboratories doing the sample analysis.

First laboratory inspects the packaging.

Second laboratory inspects the filling.

Once the inspection lot is created  the sample is calculated. Based on my sample drawing procedure two samples were created as shown in the picture bellow (QE51N). 

Best regards, M.

entry.title: BOM recursive error
entry.content:
Hi all,

I was creating a new BOM, header is A and B is a component, and I faced error message 'BOM is recursive".

Material A and B both do not have BOM in my plant now.

As I checked, other plant has a BOM, B is header and A is component.

Can other plant's BOM affect the process to make BOM for my plant, like this?

If yes, recursice allowed checking is only way to resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: Creating tree tables with OData V4 on SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm encountering an error while trying to create the service binding.


Error during compilation. See next line for details.Error in entity 'Z_C_EMPLOYEE_DATA(CDS)': Hierarchy for view Z_C_EMPLOYEE_DATA is not supported

Does anyone know what might be causing this issue? I've followed all the steps exactly as instructed.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: native sql question
entry.content:
":" must be placed right before the global variable name (e.g. use "A.MANDT = :SY-MANDT" instead of "A.MANDT =: SY-MANDT")

EXEC SQL PERFORMING APPEND_ITAB.
  SELECT A.CARRID, A.CONNID
  INTO :GS_WA-CARRID, :GS_WA-CONNID
  FROM SFLIGHT A
  WHERE A.MANDT = :SY-MANDT AND A.CARRID = :GV_CARRID
ENDEXEC.

NB: Please use the buttons "..." and "</>" to display your code nicely, and also to let the people test and fix your code easily. See how to do it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-code-in-sap-community-gt-2024/qaq-p/13684774

NB: Please indicate your database software if it's a question about native SQL ("sql error 257" depends on the database software).

entry.title: Re: Error during provisioning SAP AI Core Instance
entry.content: Hi @MarioDeFelipe, thanks for responding. I have it as free in the entitlements of the subaccounts.

entry.title: Re: Read SFTP Sender Excel file sheetname in SAP CPI
entry.content: Hi, what is the poi version that is compatible currently with CPI? and which jar files that needed to import other than these (poi.jar, poi-examples.jar ,poi-excelant.jar ,poi-ooxml.jar ,poi-scratchpad.jar) ? because facing some errors

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Identity Services - IPS ( How to Assign default groups and Unassign groups on Conditio
entry.content:
@ThomasRauen 

I didn't get your exact match from your above example described, what is X, Y, Z, YZ represents ?? 

Have you tried valueMappings?? please have a look at the sample expressions documented

entry.title: New Machine Learning and NLP features in SAP HANA Cloud 2024 Q4
entry.content:
With the SAP HANA Cloud 2024 Q4 release, several new embedded Machine Learning / AI and NLP functions have been released with the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL). Key new capabilities to be highlighted are introduction of text analysis, text vectorization, vector data as input to many more Machine Learning functions like classification and regression in the SAP HANA Cloud database. An enhancement summary is available in the What’s new document for SAP HANA Cloud database 2024.40 (QRC 4/2024).

Text analysis and text vectorization

Text chunking

Text data or documents, for further analysis typically requires to be broken down into smaller, more manageable segments such as sentences, paragraphs, or semantic units. This facilitates improved subsequent natural language processing (NLP) tasks. Moreover, processing text units by embedding models and Large Language Models (LLMs) could result in information loss or inaccuracies, as these models often have text token length limits for processing. Hence, text chunking ensures efficient text processing, mitigating potential issues.

A new text chunking function is introduced in the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), which supports multiple splitting methods

  • Splitting by character
  • Recursive splitting by a list of separators or words
  • Splitting by document type or language

For text chunking a SQL function as well as new method in the Python ML client is made available:

The new text splitting function are explained in more detail in the following blog post: Text chunking - an exciting new NLP function in SAP HANA Cloud, by @xinchen .

Text analysis

A key technique in Natural Language Processing (NLP) is text analysis, aiming to automatically extract information like a sentiment expressed or detected entities from unstructured text data.

In SAP HANA Cloud a new text analysis function within the Predictive Analysis Library is now introduced, which utilizes tiny BERT NLP models to process the text data for the following tasks

  • Named Entity Recognition (NER), for identifying entities like names of persons, organizations, locations, time expressions, etc.,
  • Sentiment analysis, for determining the expressed positive, negative, or neutral sentiment in a document, sentence of text phrase,
  • Part-of-Speech (POS) tagging, for identifying the grammatical category or words like nouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs. It is essential for comprehending the syntactic structure of languages, and can benefit many further NLP scenarios like two text analysis tasks mentioned above, i.e. NER and sentiment analysis

The text analysis task to be applied for each text object, can be specified using the task-column (e.g. ‘pos, ner, sentiment-phrase-score’) of the input table to the PAL_TEXT_Analysis function:

A more detailed introduction about the new capabilities is provided in the following blog post New Text Analysis in SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), by @likun_hou.

Text analysis in SAP HANA Cloud supports initially the following five languages: English, German, French, Spanish, and Portuguese.

Text vectorization

In recent years innovations in the field of NLP, the use of deep neural networks has emerged as an efficient technique for processing non-structured texts, as revealed by the power of large language models (LLMs). Specifically, texts can be mapped into a high-dimensional latent space by various text embedding models. Text embedding models can map semantically similar or distinct texts as numerically neighboring or far apart in the text embedding vector space. Embedding vectors, as structured numerical data, are therefore the best choice for encoding the semantic meaning of texts in applications that include similarity search, classification, regression or clustering.

SAP HANA Cloud introduces new text vectorization capabilities by use of new text embedding functions (PAL_TEXTEMBEDDING, and the SQL function VECTOR_EMBEDDING), exposing the use of a specific Text Embedding Model in SAP HANA Cloud. This is a big step toward, making text processing smoother and more efficient, right where your text data lives — with the database.

Text embedding vectors are stored in SAP HANA Cloud columns of type REAL_VECTOR, depending of analysis task and purpose the embedding vectors are used, additional techniques may be applied (discussed further below) to reduce the dimensionality of the vector data, reducing the in-memory footprint for storage and processing.

For a more detailed capability description see the following blog post Text Embedding Service in SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), by @likun_hou.

 

Enabling access to the in-database Natural Language Processing capabilities

As part of the SAP HANA Cloud instance provisioning and configuration steps, the Provisioned NLP model services in SAP HANA Cloud, the advanced settings capability “Natural Language Processing (NLP)” can be selected to enable the use of the text embedding model and the text analysis models. Further details are given in the following SAP NOTE 3527706.

 

Text search and text-embedding based advanced text mining

Keyword-based text search

Information retrieval from text typically starts with text search techniques. A new keyword-based search function BM25 search (SEARCH_DOCS_BY_KEYWORDS) is provided with the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL). BM25, is a widely used ranking functions in text search, it estimates the relevance of documents compared to a list of search keywords provided. It builds on top of term-/document-frequencies (TF_IDF values).

The new keyword-based search function is explained with more detail in the following blog post: New information retrieval techniques in SAP HANA Cloud using BM25 and ANNS for Advanced Text Mining, @xinchen . 

Text mining and -search utilizing text embeddings

Text embedding vectors allow to identify matching documents by similarity or distance in the  high-dimensional vector space. Approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) techniques, scale this task by handling similarity approximation with large scale voluminous vectorized text context stores.

The new embedding-based advanced text mining function (approximate nearest neighbor search) applies the Flat IVF algorithm, grouping the original number of vectors into a smaller number of clusters and during search, comparing the search vectors similarity to the centers of the grouped clusters. The PAL ANN search models moreover are instantiated as stateful in-memory models. By applying approximation and stateful in-memory search models, providing a much faster response in search predictions.

 

The new advanced text mining function is explained with more detail in the following blog post: New information retrieval techniques in SAP HANA Cloud using BM25 and ANNS for Advanced Text Mining, by @xinchen 

 

Unlocking insights from your text data with Machine Learning functions

Vector dimensionality reduction

Embedding vectors, are based on a high-dimensional complex datatype, often composed of of 768, 1536 or more dimensions (numeric elements), hence imposing a significant storage and processing footprint to be considered. Not all application scenarios require the original scale vector, but can benefit from dimensionality reduction techniques like Principal Component Analysis (PCA). PCA is an efficient technique to extract a lower dimensional space of variance representing component (new “vector space”), by selecting smaller number of components of e.g. 200, 100, 64, etc. compared to the dimensionality of the input vector data type.

 

The new vector dimension reduction function in the Predictive Analysis Library VECPCA, support REAL_VECTOR data input and provides the output vector of same type by lower dimensionality. The CATPCA PAL function has been enhanced to support vector as input data in mix with other features.

For a more detailed in depth use case description see the following blog post Dimensionality Reduction of Text Embeddings for Hybrid Prediction Data, by @likun_hou.

Vector data clustering

Identifying groups of similar vectors is one common use case approach for e.g. identifying groups of similar text documents by utilizing their text embedding vectors. Text analysis and term-frequency analysis task can then provide simple and out of the box techniques to describe the groups by their common or discriminating terms or entities in SAP HANA Cloud.

In addition to DBSCAN and HDBSCAN, the current release add support for vector clustering using KMEANS.

The example above shows the KMEANS clustering of an reduced vector by applying CATPCA before, and visualizing the clusters Ids mapped to the PAL TSNE components of the text embedding vector (see example below).

Another more detailed use case description can be found in the following blog post Document Clustering using KMeans and Text Embeddings., by @likun_hou. 

 

Vector data for classification and regression tasks

With the use of vector data for machine learning processing, the semantic understanding of your text data can now be unlocked as vector attributes or in mix with classic numeric or categorial feature attributes. Such hybrid models, which combine text-embeddings with structured features, demonstrate superior prediction ML models due to their ability to leverage rich contextual information from both textual and structured data.

The PAL functions HGBT, MLP_MULTI_TASK for classification, regression along with the PARTITION and MLP_RECOMMENDER functions are now enabled to process and fully exploit the value of your text vector data, to build even better prediction models.

 

The example above shows an example of the potential benefit of utilizing text embedding vectors (and reduced VECPCA “vectors”), in a example of classifying car complaints categories. The classification accuracy is greatly improved by unlocking the insights from the complaints texts and their embedding vectors.

For a more in depth use case description see the following blog post Hybrid Prediction with Tabular and Text Inputs using Hybrid Gradient Boosting Trees, by @likun_hou.

However, especially for these classification, regression task scenarios, reducing the text-embedding vectors high dimensionality to a lower dimensional PCA-vectors may provide a very significant runtime benefit (much smaller memory footprint and performance), while maintaining a maximum of the text embeddings contextual insight. Hence it is clearly recommended to validate text embedding vector preprocessing using VECPCA or CATPCA.

 

AutoML and ML pipelines enhancements

New automated outlier detection for regression operation during AutoML optimization

  • After regular AutoML optimization, outlier detection probes different outlier search configurations on best pipeline found before
  • Outlier detection method chosen is regressor dependent
  • New best pipeline(s), cross-validated and evaluated for improved scoring metrics
  • This new capability is explained with more details in this blog post Outlier detection with SAP HANA Cloud PAL AutoML regression models, by @yangzhi .

An new blog post has been added to overview machine learning model and predictions explainability for PAL AutoML and pipeline models, by @yangzhi .

Further classification, regression enhancements

Enhanced Multi-task MLP Neural Network modeling

  • Vector data-feature support for classification, regression models
  • Support for online training, update / finetune model continuously with new data
  • Supports model state for fastest prediction

 

Python ML client (hana-ml) enhancements

The full list of new methods and enhancements with hana_ml 2.23  is summarized in the changelog for hana-ml 2.23 as part of the documentation. The key enhancements in this release include

AutoML and pipeline modeling improvements

  • Massive AutoML progress monitor support
  • Enhanced AutoML time series explainability
  • Enhanced pipeline report with parallel_coordinates chart.

 New text- and vector processing PAL methods

  • New Hierarchical DBSCAN (HDBSCAN) clustering, Vector support
  • New TextSplitter
  • New PALEmbeddings for in-database text embedding generation
  • New ANNSModel for advanced text mining and approximate search
  • New BM25 search by keyword
  • Vector support KMEANS, CATPCA, HGBT, MLP_MULTI_TASK, Partition

 Further misc. enhancements

  • New create_remote_source for SQL on Files scenarios
  • Integrated unified report with time series report.
  • Enhanced CAP generation for AdditiveModelForecast when disable_hana_execution is on.

 

You can find an examples notebook illustrating the highlighted feature enhancements here 24QRC04_2.23.ipynb and 24QRC04_2.23_unlocking_text_data.ipynb.

entry.title: Restrictions for the 'Clear Manually' button in the Supplier Account Monitor view.
entry.content:
Could you please advise if it is at all possible to remove access on manually clearing a supplier invoice from a supplier account only but keeping all other access such as being able to make manual payments, add payment blocks to a specific supplier invoice etc.? It is simply the functionality of 'clearing' a supplier invoice which we want restricting.
Area in question:
1) Payables
2) Supplier Accounts
3) Going into the supplier ledger --> "Clear Manually" function


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restrictions for the 'Clear Manually' button in the S...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Unfortunately, there is no standard settings specific for this button 'clear manually' as per the current design. It is very recommended that you post this new system functional requirements in our forums, and our development team will consider and discuss if improvements can be made in the future. 

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/886

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Why the Storage location field is not in sales order overview tab
entry.content: Please try to change it to Basic settings or your can create your own variant with storage location

entry.title: Re: Can user has user defined header/item condition in Sa...
entry.content: Hello

entry.title: BTP Cloud Foundry trial - InsufficientResources Error
entry.content:
Hello all , 
I'm getting Insufficient Resources issue in my trial account from yesterday, I'm trying to deploy normal angular app NOTHING is getting deployed.

I already checked the memory and quota , they are sufficient.

Is this a new issue? Anyone else facing the same today?
